Inside My Instagram Ad Feed

About this data

Every time an ad appeared on my Instagram feed, I logged it using a Google Form. I recorded details like the ad format, category, whether I interacted with it, and how interested I was on a scale of 1 to 5. This story explores the patterns in the 42 ads I observed.

What were the ads trying to sell me?

Bar chart showing Entertainment was the most common ad category observed, followed by Home/Lifestyle and Beauty/Skincare

Entertainment ads dominated my feed, making up over 21% of all ads observed. This was followed by Home/Lifestyle and Beauty/Skincare categories.

Did the format change how interested I was?

Boxplot comparing interest scores across Image, Video, and Story style ad formats

Video ads tended to score higher on interest compared to image or story-style ads, suggesting movement captures attention more effectively.

When was I scrolling?

Histogram showing the distribution of ad observations by hour of day

Most of my observations happened in the evening hours, reflecting when I typically use Instagram after the day.

Which ads actually got my attention?

Bar chart showing interaction rates by ad category

Despite Entertainment being the most common category, it did not necessarily get the most interaction. This raises questions about whether frequency equals effectiveness.
